rationale: This is a textbook UAC bypass via sdclt.exe combined with steganographic payload delivery. EV-001 and EV-002 show PowerShell writing a malicious command and empty DelegateExecute value to HKU\...\Folder\shell\open\command, which is the classic sdclt/control.exe UAC bypass registry hijack. A0 confirms sdclt.exe was then launched at High integrity level from cmd.exe, and EV-003 shows sdclt.exe spawning control.exe, which in turn (EV-004) spawns PowerShell with '-ep bypass -window hidden' executing steganographic code that reads pixel data from 'monkey.png' to reconstruct and IEX-execute a hidden payload — a sophisticated in-memory execution technique. EV-005 shows the registry key being cleaned up afterward, indicating deliberate anti-forensic tradecraft. The full chain — registry hijack, UAC bypass, steganographic payload extraction, and cleanup — is unambiguously malicious.
